target.name: Altos Labs
target.founder: Hal Barron, Rick Klausner, Hans Bishop

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Altos Labs is a late-stage biotech company founded by Hal Barron, Rick Klausner, and Hans Bishop that has raised $3.0B to date. The valuation universe for Biotech & Health includes 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics, though none provide sufficient overlap on stage, scale, or disclosed metrics for direct peer-median triangulation.

Stage-progression analysis, the only quantitatively applicable method, uses the $3.0B total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement, yielding $3.6B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be deployed because Altos Labs does not disclose revenue; while the Biotech & Health sector median trades at 5.8x P/S, this requires revenue input. Peer-median comparison is similarly constrained—the 16 same-sector peers lack sufficient overlap in stage, valuation tier, or disclosed financials to generate a reliable median.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.1B to $4.1B, with a median of $3.6B. This range reflects modest upside from capital deployment, constrained by limited public disclosure from the target and absence of tightly comparable late-stage biotech peers with matching metrics. Key uncertainties include unrevealed revenue and clinical-stage positioning relative to the peer set, both of which would materially inform multiples-based valuation.
